blocks.text: Prepared by Mark Todd, Principal Engineer. Original to the personnel file, copy to the employee.

Charles Olson joins Brackenridge Civil Group Inc as Design Technician in the Engineering group. He came up on the board. That is worth setting down in the record because it is becoming uncommon and because it shows in the work. A man who has drawn a profile by hand understands what the profile is for. The software has not yet found a way to teach that, and I am not persuaded it is trying.

His assignment is plan production across our grading, stormwater, roadway, and utility work, to the office CAD standard. I expect he will find the standards agreeable; they were written by people who also came up on the board, and they are stricter than they need to be for that reason. Where he finds one of them wrong, he is to tell me so directly. I would far rather hear it from the man who has drawn the sheet than from a reviewing agency in a comment letter.

One expectation I am putting in writing, because it is the reason I hired him. Charles Olson would rather walk the site than take the survey file's word for what is out there. That instinct is correct, and I do not want it trained out of him by schedule pressure. When a plan looks too tidy to be the ground, it is generally the plan that is wrong and not the ground. He is authorized to go and look, on his own judgment, without clearing it with me first. Site time is booked to the job like any other hour. What I ask in return is this: when he comes back with something the survey did not show, it is written down the same day and it goes in the project folder. Short and concrete, in whatever words he would use to say it out loud, is exactly the right length. A note nobody wrote is a note this firm does not have, and we find that out at the worst possible moment or not at all.

The rest is ordinary. Files stay in the lettered project folder on the share. Time is recorded against a job number daily. Nothing goes out under seal that I have not read. I have said all of this to Charles Olson in person; it is set down here so that it is on the record, and so that he can hold me to the part about walking the site.
